NISBET, Hugh Adair.—B. 1873; ed. at Winchester Coll.; served in S. African war (Queen’s medal and two clasps); pub. prosecutor, Transvaal, 1902; asst. res. mag., Transvaal, 1903; pol. mag. and registrar of supreme ct., Grenada, 1907; registr. of supreme ct., Hong Kong, 1911.
NOAKS, Benjamin.—B. 1866; ed. London and Cambridge; M.A., 1896; principal Gill Coll., 1895; high schl., Queenstown, 1898; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1900-03; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., July, 1909.

NOALL, W. E.—B.1880; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Jan., 1911; conf. clk., 1st Mar., 1916; minor staff clk., 8th Sept., 1917; sec. to visual instr. comtee., 1st Jan., 1908.

NOBLE, Peter, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Elgin and Aberdeen, Scotland; asst. civ. engrnr., P.W.D., Cyprus, 1906; ag. dist. engrnr. in 1907 and 1909; dist. engrnr., Demerara, B. Guiana, Aug., 1910; ag. in addition as chief draughtman, 1911, and as ast. col. civ. engrnr. in 1912; col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., Dominica, Aug., 1912; nom. mem., Roseau Town Board, offl. mem. legis. coun. and road board.
NORMAN, James Edward Lynch.—B. 1861; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1st May, 1878; chief clk., post office, 9th Dec., 1889.
NORRIS, C. G.—B. 1890; ed. St. Catherine’s Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; cadet, Solomon Islands, Jan., 1915.

NORRIS, Hon. Tobias C.—B. 1861; farmer; elec. to legis. ass., Manitoba, 1896 and 1899; defeated, 1903; re-elec., 1907, 1910, 1914, and 1915; leader of opposition for several years; premier, May, 1915.

NORTH, Frederic Dudley, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1866; ed. at Rugby; clk. lands off., W. Aust., 1887; clk. of the ex. coun., 1891, and priv. sec. to Sir John Forrest, G.C.M.G.; when premier of W. Aust., 1891-1901; rep. W. Aust. as member of royal celebrations commonwealth comtee. in Melbourne on occasion of opening of first commonwealth parlmt. by H.R.H. the Duke of Cornwall and York, May, 1901; under-sec., col. sec.’s dept., 1902; also comprtr.-gen. of prisons from 1912.

NORTH, Roland Arthur Charlie.—B. 1889; ed. Blundell’s and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. cadet, Hong Kong civil serv., 16th Nov., 1912; ast. cable censor, Aug. to Nov., 1914; passed Cantonese, Feb., 1915; ag. 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, Apr., 1915; in charge of pass office, July, 1915; ag. 1st clk., pol. magistracy, in addition to other duties, Mar., 1916.

NORTHCOTE, Geoffrey Alexander Stafford.—Ed. at Blundell’s Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (honours); and Newte and Huish exhibn.; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 27th May, 1904; dist. commr., 1909.

NORTHEY, Major-General Sir Edward, K.C.M.G., C.B.—B. 1868; served with Hazara expdn., 1891; Miranzei expdn., 1891; Iszai expdn., 1892; in S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyassa and Rhodesia field force, 1915-18; lieut.-col. (brevet col.), 1915; King’s R.R.C., 1911; gov. E. Africa Prot. and high commr., Zanzibar Prot., 15th July, 1918; assumed govt., 31st Jan., 1919.

NORTON-TRAILL, Henry Lionel (formerly Smith), F.R.G.S., F.S.A. (Scotland).—Capt. 4th batt. H.L.I.; late lieut. 35th I.Y., S. African field force; S. African War medal and 2 clasps; ast.-res., N. Nigeria, 1904; served as polit. offr. with expdn. against Lakai cannibals, 1905; with expdn. against Munchi tribes, 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1911; author of “Armorial of the County of Orkney.”

NOTLEY, W. K. D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1890; ed. at Sherborne Schl.; served with S. African constab., 1901-1907 (Queen’s medal and five clasps); deputy inspr.-gen. of police, Uganda, 19th June, 1907; comsnr. of police, 1st Aug., 1908; commr. of police, E. Africa Prot., 10th Dec., 1908; awarded King's police medal, 1918.

NOTT-BOWER, WILLIAM GUY.—B. 1890; B.A. Oxon.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Kandy Kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Apr., 1915; transf'd. to secretariat of bd. of inland rev., England, June, 1916.

NOVA SCOTIA, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. CLARENDON LAMB WOBBELL, M.A., D.C.L., D.D.; B. 1853; headmaster, Gananoque high schl., 1879; ditto, Brockville coll. inst., 1882; curate, Gananoque, 1881; Brockville, 1882; rector, Morrisburg, 1884; ditto, St. Marks, Barriefield, 1891; prof. of English, R.M.C., Kingston, 1891; man. chap., Bishop of Ontario, 1896; arch-deacon of Ontario, 1900; rector, St. Luke's, Kingston, 1903; consecrated Bishop of Nova Scotia, 1904; prolocutor, prov. synod of Canada, 1904.

NUGENT, GEORGE OLIVER.—2nd elk., col. sec.'s office, Leeward Ilds., Nov., 1893; ag. 1st elk., Dec., 1894, to June, 1895; on reorganisation of office became 3rd elk., Apr., 1897; ag. 2nd elk. and ex. co., Apr. to Dec., 1897, and June, 1899, to Aug., 1900; ag. ch. elk., and elk. fed. ex. and leg. co., and Antigua leg. co., Aug., 1900; ag. priv. sec., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898, Jan. to June, 1899, Oct., 1899, to Aug., 1900; 2nd elk., July, 1901; priv. sec., Nov., 1900, to July, 1901; lieut.-defence force, Aug., 1901; M.I. certif., 1901; A.D.C. to gov., 1903; ag. ast. col. sec., and elk. to exec. and legis. couns., Mar. to Oct., 1905; passed musketry, Hythe, 1906; resigned Antigua def. force to join militia; capt. 3rd Scottish Rifles, 8th June, 1906; seconded as ast. sec., N. Nigeria, 22nd Sept., 1906.

NUGENT, OLIVER.—Mag.'s elk., Antigua, 1867; elk., treasy., 1868; ret., 1871; mag. and man. of Barbuda, 1900 to 1903; ag. supplementary mag., dep. coroner, and escheator-gen., Antigua, and mag., dist. L (Barbuda), 1903; confirmed, 1908; lieut.-col. in commd. of Antigua Yeomanry Calvary for 5 yrs.; pres. of Antigua defence reserve, 1905-1909; chmn., bd. of guardians, 1906; offl. M.L.C., Antigua, 1908; and dep. chmn. of St. John's city comnrs., 1910.

NUNAN, JOSEPH JOHN.—Ed. Univ. Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; fellow of roy. univ. of Ireland, Blake hist. schlr. and 1st vice-chancellor's prizeman, Dublin Univ.; 1st mod. language schlr., roy. univ., Ireland; Bacon schlr., Gray's Inn; gold medallist in history, literature and composition; won exhibitn. and cert. of honour at honour examn. for call to the bar, King's Inn, 1898; H.M.'s vice-consul, and ch. judicial offr., B.C. Africa Prot., 12th Oct., 1899; judge of high ct., B.C.A., 11th Aug., 1902; also mem. of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for Eastern Africa; draughtsman, Brit. Cen. Africa Prot., 1900-5; pres., native land comnr., Mar.-July, 1903; specially employed in connection with native disturbances, Cent. Angoniland, Jan.-Feb., 1901, and at other times specially employed; solr.-gen., B. Guiana, 1905; ag. puisne judge, Mar. to July, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1906, to Feb., 1907; ag. special puisne judge, Nov., 1906, atty.-gen., B. Guiana, 1912.
NUNAN, P. F., M.B., Bao.-Surg., M.D.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

NUNN, BERNARD.—B.A. Keble Coll., Oxon.; b. 1876; cadet S.S., Nov., 1900; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Apr., 1905; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1905; 4th mag., Singapore, Aug., 1906, but cont. to act at Alor Gajah; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1908; dep. regiar., sup. ct., Aug., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, July, 1910; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Jan., 1912; continued to act as dist. offr., Butterworth; ag. offl. assignee, Singapore, 11th Apr., 1912; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, 16th May, 1914; ag. dist. judge, regiar. and mag., Malacca, 30th Sept., 1914; offr. of cls. III, F.M.S., 13th Dec., 1917; continued as regiar., dist. judge and mag., Singapore.

NYLANDER, A. W. M.—Apptd. temporary elk., col. sec.'s office, S. Leone, Aug., 1889; asst. police elk., May, 1891; 2nd elk., Crown law off., June, 1896; 1st elk., July, 1896; 1st elk., col. sec.'s off., Dec., 1897; chief elk., Mar., 1898; ag. charity comnrs., Sept., 1898; J.P., July, 1899; ag. ast. col. sec., May, 1901; and again from May, 1902, to end of year; charity comnrs., Apr., 1902.

O'BRIEN, ANDREW, S.M.—B. 1873; indentured elk., publ. hosp., Bahamas, June, 1889; 3rd elk. gen. post off., March, 1893; elk. registr. of records dept., March, 1897; customs off., Apr., 1901; ag. res. just. and rev. offr., Ragged Is., Feb. to May, 1902; ditto Rum Cay, June to Dec., 1902; elk., police ot., Jan., 1903; res. just., Ragged Island, July, 1903; res. just., San Salvador, July, 1905; Out Island comnrs. (3rd div.), 1909.

O'BRIEN, LT.-COL., C.R.M., C.M.G. (1908).—B.1859; ed. Felsted, on the Continent, and Royal Mil. Coll. (passed out with honours); entd. Army as 2nd lieut., 1878; lieut., 1880; capt., 1887; major, 1897; brev. lt.-col., 1902; 2nd in command of 1st E. Lancs. regt., 30th Oct., 1902; served in India from 1880 to 1895; retrd. on pension in order to remain under C.O., 4th Mar., 1903; served in S. African War, Jan., 1900, to end of campaign; pres. of mil. tribunal, 1900-1901; S. African War medal and 3 clasp, King's medal and 2 clasps (twice mentioned in despatches); senior dep. comnrs. Transvaal town pol., J.P. for the Witwatersrand, and in charge of mtd. pol., 1st May, 1901; in charge of martial law arrangements, Johannesburg and Rand, and personal ast. to comnrs., 1st May, 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. comnrs., Transvaal town pol., and J.P. for Transvaal, 6th May to 8th Nov., 1904; deputy comnrs., headqrs., Transvaal town pol., Oct., 1905, to 30th June, 1908; ag. comnrs., Transvaal town pol., and J.P. for Transvaal, 1st Jan. to 22nd July, 1907; deputy comnrs., Transvaal town pol.; retrenched, 1908; col. sec., Gambia, 8th Jan., 1910; ag. gov., Gambia, 6th July to 10th Dec., 1910, 18th Aug. to 18th Dec., 1911, and from 17th Aug. to 26th Oct., 1912; gov., Seychelles, 7th Nov., 1912; gov., Barbados, 1st June, 1918; assumed govt., 27th Sept., 1918.

O'BYRNE, HUGH MORGAN.—Chief of customs, Somaliland Prot., 22nd Feb., 1901; regiar. of shipping, 6th Mar., 1905; specially commended by S. of S. on two occasions; brought to notice of govt. for sea-transport and harbour work by Gen. Sir C. Egerton in his desps. on Somaliland campaign, 1902-4; G.A.S. medal and clasp, Somaliland, 1902-1904; reed. thanks of army council for assistance in compilation of military report on Somaliland, 1907; clasp, G.A.S. medal, Somaliland, 1908-1910.
O'CONNOR, ARTHUR DENNIS.—B. 1869; crown solicitor, Trinidad, 15th Jan., 1900.

O'CONNOR, HUGH JOSEPH.—B. 1887; Lieut., Gambia Artillery (V.), 1910; mem. of navigation and pilotage board, Gambia, 1914; ast. comnrs. of police, G. Coast, Jan., 1916; ag. staff instructor, Mar., 1916; ag. commr. of police, Sept., 1917; ag. staff instructor, June, 1918.
ORR, J. R.—Director of eductn., E.A.P., Mch., 1911.

ORR, HON. THOMAS, C.M.G. (1912).—3rd cls. clk., chan. branch of exchqr. and audit dept., 11th Jan., 1875; examr., 2nd sect., 16th Aug., 1885; 1st sect., 31st Jan., 1894; 2nd cls. clk., 2nd sect., 10th June, 1901; auditor-gen., Natal, 16th Aug., 1902; ret. 1910; elected to Union parlmt. as M.L.A. for Pietermaritzburg North; pub. debt comsnr., 1913; min. of finance, 1918.

OSBORN, JOHN FREDERICK.—B. 1865; served articles with R. Fabian Russell, F.R.I.B.A., of London; ast. engrnr. on construction, Manchester Ship Canal, Nov., 1887, to Dec., 1891; attached to P.W.D., Jamaica, July, 1892, to Nov., 1896, on surveys for and construction of bridges; ag. irrigation engrnr., Rio Cobre canals; col. survr., and inapr. of P. W., Turks and Caicos Islds., Nov., 1896; J.P., 1901; ag. ast. comsnr., Salt Cay, Apr., 1902, to July, 1903, and from May to Oct., 1904; transf. to lands and wks. dept., Fiji, Nov., 1904; 2nd ast. comsnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1906; ast. comsnr. of works, Jan., 1908; dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1911.

OSBORNE, GEORGE HAROLD.—Ed. at Leatherhead and Pembroke Coll., Camb. (scholar); Goldsmith's exhib.; B.A. (honours) in classics, 1898; S. African War, 1901-1902; medal and 5 clasps; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th Feb., 1906; dist. comsnr., 1910.
OSBORNE, RICHARD BOURKE.—Cadet, S. Sttlmte., Nov., 1909; ag. dep. regt. of sup. et., Penang, May, 1910.

O'SHAUGHNESSY, JOHN J. F., M.Inst.E.E.—Major, Lon. Army troops, R.E.; served in S. Africa as capt. R.E., 1900-1902 (2 medals and 5 clasps); hon. capt. in the Army; ast. supt., telegraph construction, S. Nigeria, 1905; dep. director of telegraphs, S. Nigeria, 1907.
O'SULLIVAN, HON. THOS. K.C.—B. 1856; solr., Queensland, 1878; called to the bar; sec. for pub. wks., Queensland, 1907; min. of agric., with a seat in legis. coun., Feb., 1908; afterwards atty.-gen.

OSWELL, WILLIAM ST. JOHN, F.R.G.S.—Ed. at Oswestry gram. schl. and by private tutor; lieut. 2nd batt. sharpshooters, Boer war, 1901-2 (medal and 5 clasps); ast. comsnr., S. Leone, 1903; comsnr., Karene dist., S. Leone Prot., 1906.
OTTOLE, W. F.—Entd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1893; 2nd clk., post office, 1904; Out Island comsnr. (2nd div.), 1909; 2nd cls. supervisor of customs, G. Coast; supervisor of customs, S. Nigeria, 1912.
OTTERTSON, HENRY, C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1846; apptd. to the staff of H. of Rep., N. Zealand, 1872; clk. of H. of Rep. 1898-1915.

OTTLEY, Rear-Admiral Sir CHARLES LANGLEY, K.C.M.G. (1907); C.B. (1911); M.V.O. (1903).—B. 1838; Peru, 1877; Egypt, 1882; has served as naval attache to maritime cts. at Washington, Tokio, Rome, St. Petersburg, and Paris; dir. of naval intell., 1905-1907; sec. to comtee. of Imp. defence, 1907; resig., 1912.

OTWAY, J. H.—Ck., treas., Grenada, 1897; 2nd audit clk., 1899; audit clk., St. Vincent, 1901; audit clk., St. Lucia, Jan., 1916; sub-collector and dist. offr., Soufriere; J.P. for the colony; cable censor, St. Vincent, 1918; chief clk., govt. office, and clk. of councils, St. Vincent, 1918; has held numerous acting appts.

OVERY, CHAS. RAND.—Educ. at Yalding gram. schl.; ch. architectural draughtsman, Admiralty wks. dept., Keyham naval barracks, 1903-4; ch. draughtsman, survey dept., E.A.P., 1904-6; govt. architect, P.W.D., E.A.P., 15th July, 1908.

OWEN, GEORGE DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 16th Jan., 1907; 3rd cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1915; ag. 2nd and conf. clk., Oct., 1915 to May, 1916; ast. sec., local defence comtee., 1914-1915; ag. sec., ditto, 1915-1916; sec. to educn. coman., June, 1914 to Nov., 1916; sec., West Indian Court of Appeal Comfee., 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. S. W. Knaggs in 1910, 1912 and 1916; to Sir G. R. Le Hunte, 1913-1915; to Sir J. R. Chancellor, 1st June, 1916; serving as lieut. with H.M. Forces, since Dec., 1916.

OWEN, HAROLD ALBERT.—B. 1892; entd. crown agt.'s office, Dec., 1911; seconded as 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, July, 1916; sec. to comsnr. of enquiry into condition of St. John's fire brigade, Nov., 1916.

OWEN-SMITH, G.—B. 1868; entd. Cape civ. serv. (customs dept.), 1888; passed civ. serv. law exam., 1895; sec., tariff comtee., Bloemfontein customs comfee., 1903; sec., Cape customs tariff coman., 1906; customs adviser, Pietermaritzburg customs comfee., 1906; ditto, Pretoria and Capetown comfee., 1908; sec., commerce and industries comsnr., 1910; dep. comsnr. of customs and excise, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1914; comsnr. to enquire into prices of foodstuffs, etc., 1916; ag. comsnr. of customs and excise, 1918.
PADDLE, JAMES ISAAC.—F.R.C.S. (Eng.); M.D. (Lond.); ast. govt. med. offr. and comsnr. in lunacy, Mauritius 8th Sept., 1890; med. supt., lunatic asylum, 15th June, 1898.

PAGDEN, A.S., C.M.G. (1917).—Ed. Wellington Coll. and King's Coll., Camb., scholar of King's Coll., 1stclass, classical tripod, 1881; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1881; pol. mag., Panadure, June, 1887; office ast. to the govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1889; dist. judge, Badulla, 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1896; ag. comsnr. of requests Colombo, June, 1896; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1900; chmn., municipal coun., and mayor of Colombo, June, 1901; prin. ast. col. sec., Feb., 1906; postmr.-gen., Nov., 1906; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., Aug., 1911; postmr.-gen., Oct., 1911; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., Feb., 1913; ag. controller of revenue, Aug., 1913; controller of revenue, Nov., 1913; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1915; controller of revenue, April, 1916; custodian of enemy property, in addition to own duties, Aug., 1916; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1918.
PAGDEN, N. D.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912.

PAGE, GEORGE WM.—B. 1861; copyist, science and art dept., S. Kensington museum, Apr., 1881, to Dec., 1884; 2nd cl. clk. prsn. dept., Jan., 1885, to Dec., 1896; keeper of the gaol Freetown, S. Leone, Jan., 1897, to Apr., 1901; ast. dist. comsnr., S. Leone Prot., May, 1901; dist. comsnr., Oct., 1903; dist. comsnr. of Sherbro dist., Aug., 1905, and in addition dist. comsnr. of N. Sherbro dist., Mar., 1906; ag. curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1900, to Apr., 1901; is J.P. for the colony.

PAINE, JOHN JAMES.—B. 1863; served with 19th (Princess of Wales's Own) Hussars in Egypt, and gained medal and 3 clasps and Khedive's star. for Suakim and Nile expedtns., 1884 and 1885; apptd. coal porter, C.O., 1st Jan., 1893; placed on estab. as office porter, 15th Apr., 1904.
PALLISER, CHARLES FREDERICK WRAY, C.M.G. (1916).—Entered civ. ser., New Zealand, 1874; sec. to high comntr. for N.Z. in London, 1909; ag. high comntr., May to Aug., 1912; ret., 1916.

PALMER, ALFRED FELIX.—Auditor's clk., Grenada, Sept., 1879; rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; ag. ch. rev. offr., 1882-83; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1883 and 1884; sub-collr. customs and inland rev., and clk., 2nd dist. ct., Oct., 1884; acted twice as mag., and as coroner, dep. coroner, and J.P., ag. ch. clk., govt. office, 1893.

PALMER, CHAS. STUART RUSSELL, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Roorkee Civil Eng. Coll., India; ent. P.W.D., India, Oct., 1878; as astt. engrnr.; spec. duty in England, 1885 to 1887; astt. to ch. engrnr., and astt. sec. to ch. comntr., cent. provs., up to 1889, when apptd. exec. engrnr. of Hoohangabad div., and in 1892 of Nagpur div.; ret. from Indian serv., May, 1894; astt. survr., W. Aust., Oct., 1894; engrnr. in charge, roads and bridges, and harbours and rivers branches, Aug., 1897, of water supply, 1898, and sewerage, 1900; engrnr.-in-chief, June, 1902; inspctg. engrnr. for W.A. in Lond., 1904.
PAMPELLONE, ARMAND JOSEPH, M.B., Ch.B. Edin.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Feb., 1906; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1913.
PANTIN, CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1853; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1877; astt. aud.-gen., 1st Apr., 1912.
PARDOE, AVERN.—B. 1845; ed. Stratford-upon-Avon Collegiate gram. schl.; on staff of Toronto Globe, 1875-89; managing editor, 1883-9; librarian of Ontario legislature, 1898.

PARKER, JAMES T.—Cert. teacher, Leeward Is.; dist. postmtr., Bethesda, 1901; schlrmr., govt. boys' reformatory schl., 1903; clk. and storekeeper, P.W.D., and clk. to road bd., Antigua, 1906; 1st govt. offr., Virgin Is., 1915; inspr. of weights and measures, Virgin Is., 1917.

PARKER, HON. SIR STEPHEN HENBY, K.C.M.G. (1914), KT. BACH. (1908).—Called to the bar, 1868; Q.C., 1890; mem. of legislature of W. Australia from 1878 to 1897; col. sec., 1892 to 1894; represented W. A. at the C.O., and gave evidence before sel. comtee. of H. of C. relative to the grant of self-govt. to the Colony, 1890; represented W. A. in London relative to the federation of Australia, 1900; justice of supreme ct., 1901; ch. just. of W. A., 1906-1913.

PARKER, WILLIAM ALSTON.—B. 1865. ed. Queen's coll., B. Guiana; entd. B. Guiana govt. serv., 1883; qualified as notary public and apptd., 1892; admitted solr., 1897; chief clk., and acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, Feb., 1908; transfd. as 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 19th Apr., 1909; sec., man. comttee, of imperial bureau of entomology; sec. to comttee, apptd. to investigate relationship between wild animals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914; sec. trop. diseases research fund advisory comttee.; sec. managing comttee. of trop. diseases bureau; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Mr. Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 1917; on military service from 16th May, 1915.

PARKINSON, J. W. H.—B. 1877; ed. Brighton Coll.; solicitor, sup. ct.; regisr. of high ct.; principal regisr. of documents; regisr. of companies; sec. for inventions and designs, E.A.P., 26th Mar., 1907; admstr.-gen. and offr. recvr., Prov. Seyidie, E.A.P., 26th Aug., 1909; currency comntr., E.A.P., 1909; sec., Wafk. comntr., E.A.P., 1914; custodian of enemy property, 1915.
PARNALL, J.—Asst. aud., E. Africa Prot., 1915.

PARODI, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1870; ed. Stonyhurst Coll., Lancs.; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., June, 1893; solr.-gen., N. Nigeria, 4th July, 1903; ag. atty.-gen., 2nd Aug. to 22nd Dec., 1903, 15th Apr. to 21st July, 1904, 1st Mar. to 31st Oct., 1905, 16th June to 20th Oct., 1906, 18th Nov., 1906, to 13th June, 1907, and 13th Feb. to 29th Apr., 1908; ag. chief justice, 30th Apr. to 15th Dec., 1908, and Sept., 1909; puisne judge, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. chief just., 28th Apr. to 23rd Nov., 1910, and from 21st Feb. to 20th Sept., 1912; judge of the circuit ct. of the Prot. and puisne judge of the supreme ct. of the col. of Sierra Leone, Apr., 1913; ag. chief just., 22nd Oct. to 9th Nov., 1913, 27th Nov., 1914 to 17th Apr., 1915, and from 19th July, 1916 to 17th Jan., 1917.

PARR, CECIL WILLIAM CHASE.—Jun. offr., Perak civ. ser., 1889; dist. offr., Tampin, Negri Sembilan, 1897; dist. offr., Klang, 1904; ag. comntr. of trade and customs, F.M.S., 1909; ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, 1911; 2nd lieut. M.S.V.R., 1911; under sec., F.M.S., 1912; lieut. M.S.V.R., 1912; ag. agt., M.S. information agency, London, 1912; seconded as gov. of B. N. Borneo, Apr., 1913; lieut. (remount offr.), 30th June to 7th Dec., 1915; British res., Pahang, 5th Feb., 1916.

PARSONS, ALLAN CHILCOTT, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.P.H. (Oxon.), 1910; certif. in trop. med., 1903; F.R.G.S., F.R.C.I.—Ed. Epsom, St. Thomas' hosp., and Kings' Coll., Lond.; late H.S. and H.P., Royal Free hosp., Lond.; senr. H.S., Victoria hosp. for children, Lond.; clin. astt., skin dept., St. Thomas' hosp., olin. astt., St. John's hosp., Lond.; civil surg., S. African War (Queen's medal and four clasps); joined W.A.M.S. Aug., 1903; supt., Bornu freed slaves home, 1904; serv. with Chibbuk expdttn., 1906 (medal and clasp, ment. in desps.); san. offr., G. Coast, 1914; late lieut., R.A.M.C. (V.); contributed "Arrows and Arrow Wounds in N. Nigeria," Brit. Med. Jour., 1909; "Filaria Volvulus" to "Parasitology," 1909.

PASHLEY, EDWARD REYNOLD.—B. 1869; storekeeper, petroleum warehouse, customs, Bahamas, 1897; landing waiter, 1896; senr. clk. of check staff, 1897; landing waiter and preventive offr., 1899; attended impl. customs, London, 1900; customs offr., excise supervisor, boarding offr., Tobago, 1901; landing waiter-r., customs, Trinidad, 1902; transfd. to acctnt.'s branch, treasury, 1905; 5th clk. and paymr., 1907; 4th clk., 1912; 3rd clk., 1913; comptr. of customs, regisr. of shipping, J.P., Bahamas, 1914.

PASSINGHAM, FRANK SELWOOD.—B. 1883; ed. Goldsmiths' and Morley Colls., Lond.; hon. medallist in typography, City and Guilds of Lond. Inst.; govt. printer, Mauritius, Feb., 1914; also supt., bookbinding works and stat. stores, Apr., 1914.

PATENAUBE, HON. ESIOFF LEON, B.A.—B. 1875; ed. Montreal Coll. and Laval Univ.; advocate, elected to legis. assem., Quebec, 1908; re-elec., 1912; min. of in. rev., Borden ministry, Oct., 1915; elec. by acclamation for Hochelaga, Quebec, 1915; S. of S., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada.
PATTEN, W. G.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., July, 1915.
PATTERSON, JOHN, D.S.O. (1916).—Ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; Scottish Horse, S. African War; asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1905; chief paymaster, E. Africa Pay Corps.

PATTULLO, HON. THOMAS D.—B. 1873; journalist; editor of "The Galt Reformer"; apptd. secy. to Comsrr. of the Yukon, 1897; resigned to enter business in Dawson; later, removed to Prince Rupert, where he took an active interest in municipal affairs, at one time holding the office of mayor; elec. to legis. assm., Brit. Columbia, g.e., 1916; min. of lands in Brit. Columbia cabinet, 1916.

PAUL, A. H. D.—Ed. at Bradfield and Bath colls.; trooper in Matabeleland mtd. pol., 1896; took part in the Jameson raid, 1896; planting in Ceylon, 1896 to 1904; trooper in Ceylon M.I.; dist. supt. of pol., N. Nigeria, 1906; asst. res., 1st Nov., 1908; ag. supt. of agric., Ang., 1911.
PAUL, ROBERT JAMES.—B. 1888; sub.-inspr. of constab., Trinidad, 9th Sept., 1912.

PAULIN, GEORGE.—B. 1885; ed. Canterbury Coll., Univ. of New Zealand, B.Sc., 1906; B.Eng., 1908; pupilage with govt. rly., New Zealand, with city engrnr., Christchurch, and with Selwyn county engrnr., and engrnr.-in-chief, city of Dunedin drainage; engrnr.-survr., P.W.D., N. S. Wales, 1910; dist. engrnr., Fiji, 1912; res. engrnr., Suva harbr. wks., Fiji, 1915.
PAWAN, JAMES LENOX, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1887; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1913; dist. med. offr., 8th Mar., 1916.

PEACOCK, HON. SIR ALEXANDER JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; mem. of Austn. Federal Convention, 1897-8; premier of Victoria, 1901-2; later chief sec. and min. for labour, Victoria; min. for educ. and labour, Dec., 1913; premier, treas., and min. of justice, 1914.

PEACOCK, WALTER.—B.A., Trinity Hall, Camb.; cadet, S. Stltms., Nov., 1898; passed fin. exam. Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 2nd asst. protact. Chinese, Penang, July, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Dec., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, June, 1903; apptd. to cls. V., Sept., 1903; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1906; seconded for service in Burma, June, 1907; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Mar., 1909.

PEAKE, HON. ARCHIBALD HENRY.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1897; treas. and atty-gen., 1906-1909; premier, treas., and min. of educn., 5th June to 22nd Dec., 1909; premier, comsrr. of crown lands and imgrn., and min. of educn., 22nd Dec., 1909, to 3rd June, 1910; premier, treas. and min. of educn., 1912-1915; premier, and chief sec. since 1917.

PEARCE, MAJOR FRANCIS BARROW, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1866; ed. at Cheltenham and Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; entd. W. Yorks. regt., 1886; capt., 1893; brevet-major 1900; major, 1904; served with Ashanti expedn., 1895-6 (star); in B. C. Africa, 1898 (deeps., medal with clasp); in commd. of British forces in operation in E. Nyassaland, 1899 (deeps., medal with clasp brevet major); and in S. Africa, 1901 (medal with clasp); asst. dep. comsrr., B.C. Africa Prot., 1st Nov., 1897; dep. comsrr., 1901; acted as comsrr., and consul-gen., 1908, Nov., 1905, to Jan., 1906, and during 1907; ag. gov., Nyassaland, Apr.-July, 1910; and from Nov., 1912 to Sept., 1913; British res., Zanzibar Prot., Mar., 1914; ag. high comsrr., 10th Apr., 1917.

PEARCE, HON. GEORGE FOSTER.—Senator for Western Australia in commonwealth parlmt.; sec. of trades coun. at Perth, 1896; pres., 1899; returned to first Commonwealth Senate, 1901; re-elected, 1906, and elected chmn. of comtees; min. of defence, C. of A., Apr., 1910 to June, 1913; at. imp. Conf., 1911; min. of defence since Sept., 1914.

PEARCE, THOS. WM.—B. 1855; Missionary of Lond. Miss. Soc., Canton, 1879-1894; Hong Kong, 1890-1902; ag. inspr. of schools, 5th Sept. to 9th Oct., 1888, again in 1900; examiner, Queen's Coll., 1897 and 1898; mem. of comtees on interpretation, 1899-1900; apptd. mem. of bd. of examiners in Chinese, 14th Jan., 1897; mem. of govng. body, Queen's Coll., 1905; mem. of comtees on continuation classes, 1907.

PEARSE, WILFRID WILLIAM.—M.B., C.M., Aberdeen, 1893; D.P.H., Aberdeen, 1894; M.D., Aberdeen, 1906; 2nd asst. med. offr., Shoreditch Infirmary, 1894-95; asst. med. offr. of health, Hong Kong, 1901; J.P., 1902; ag. med. offr. of health, 1903-6, 1908 and 1915; med. offr. of health, 1915; lecturer on pub. health, Univ. of Hong Kong.

PEARSON, ARTHUR A., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Rugby; apptd., 1887, after a compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols.; 2nd class clk., Sept., 1872; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Mar., 1874, to Feb., 1878; asst. priv. sec. to Sir M. Hicks-Beach, Feb. to Mar., 1878; priv. sec. to Earl Cadogan, 1879, to Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. M. E. Grant Duff, Apr., 1880, to Dec., 1880; 1st-clas clk., Jan., 1881; principal clk., Apr., 1895; repres. crown cols. at postal confere., London, June and July, 1898; one of the Brit. delegates at the Brussels sugar conference, 1901-2; asst. delegate, permanent sugar commission at Brussels, 1903; ret. from C.O., 31st Dec., 1906; accompanied Sir C. Lucas on his visit to Australia and N. Zealand, 1909.

PEARSON, AYLMER CAVENDISH, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1876; ed. at Felsted schl., Essex, and Trinity coll., Dublin; cadet, N. Borneo serv., 1897; asst. treas., 1899; res., Kudat, 1901; sec. to gov., 1904; sessions judge, 1905; mem. of coun., 1906; ag. judel. comsrr., 1908; ag. comsrr. of lands, 1908; gov't. sec. and judge of high ct., 1909; ag. gov., N. Borneo, 1910 and 1911; temporarily attached to C.O. for a short period in 1912, after which he returned to Borneo as dep. gov. for 3 months; attached to various colonial services for short periods; gov. B. N. Borneo, 1915.
PEARSON, J. M.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 14th Apr., 1909; dist. comsrr., Dec., 1917.
PEARSON, JOSEPH, D.Sc. (Liverpool), B.Sc. (Victoria), F.L.S.—Dir. of the Colombo Museum, and marine biologist to Ceylon Govt., 11th Aug., 1910.
PEASE, J. W. K.—Asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914.
PEDRAZA, R.—Asst. dist. comsrr., R.A.P., Aug., 1914.

PEEBLES, MAJOR H. W., D.S.O. (1917).—Joined Canadian M.I., Dec., 1899; 2nd lieut. A.S.C., Sept., 1900; lieut., Nov., 1901; S. African War, Jan., 1900, to May, 1902 (severely wounded, Queen's medal and four clasps, King's medal and two clasps); res. of offers., June, 1902; A.D.C. to Brig.-Gen. Sir W. H. Manning, K.C.M.G., C.B., Feb., 1903, to June, 1904; Somaliland campaign, Jan., 1903, to June, 1904 (twice men in deep, medal and two clasps); A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., gov'r of B. Hond., Oct., 1904; local capt., Nov., 1904; commdng. Belize L.I. volrs., Dec., 1904; local major, 1905; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1905 to Jan., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to Sir E. B. Sweet-Escott, K.C.M.G., gov' of Leeward Is., Oct., 1907; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1908; 3rd class res., N. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; resigned on account of ill-health, June, 1909; clk. and priv. sec. to administrator, Dominica, Mar., 1913; raised and commanded Dom. Defence For., Aug., 1914; served with B.E.F. in France from Oct., 1914; ment. in despatches, Jan., 1916, Jan., 1917, and May, 1917; D.A.D.O.S., 3rd Army, B.E.F., June 1916 to Oct., 1917; D.A.D.O.S., Aldershot Command, May, 1918 to cessation of hostilities.

PEEL, W.—B.A., Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripods., 1896; cadet, S. Sttlm'ts., Oct., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Aug.-Dec., 1898; and from Mar., 1899; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; and in Aug., 1903; ag. asst. supt., Indian immigrts., Sing., in addition, Aug., 1903; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Feb., 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Penang, Jan., 1906; dist. offr., Tampin, F.M.S. June, 1908; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, 21st Feb., 1910; ag. supt., of Indian immigrts., S.S. and F.M.S., 2nd May, 1910; ag. chief asst. dist. offr., Kinta, and regitr. of titles, South Perak, 21st Oct., 1910; seconded as pres. mun. comsrr., Penang, 1st Jan., 1911; promoted to Class III., but remained seconded, 1st Jan., 1912; acted as res. councillor, Penang, in addtn., Feb. to Oct., 1917; pres., mun. comsrr., Singapore, July, 1918.

PEET, JAMES HERBERT.—B. 1882; clk. audit office, Bahamas, 1904; 4th clk., P.O., 1907; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1909; 3rd clk., treasry. dept., 1910; ch. clk., P.O., 1913; ch. clk. and asst. exang. offr., customs, 1914; has held numerous ag. appts.

PELLETIER, HON. LOUIS PHILIPPE, K.C.—B. 1857; ed. St. Anne Coll., Laval Univ., Quebec; pres. of Nat. conservative assocn. of prov. of Quebec; founded newspaper "La Justice"; leg. coun., Quebec, 1888; res., and ret. by acclam. to legis. assembly for Dorchester; prov. sec., 1891; atty.-gen., 1896-7; sat for Dorchester until 1904; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1911; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and postmaster-gen. in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; judge, superior court, Quebec, 1914; ct. of King's Bench, 1915.

PELLETIER, PHILIPPE.—B. 1849; called to the bar, Quebec, 1875; mem. of the coun. of the bar of Montreal, and sec., 1878; a fire comsrr. for city of Montreal, 1879 to 1887; unsuccessfully contested St. John's in provincial elections, 1886, and Beauharnois in Dominion elections, 1887; ch. clk., sec. of state's dept., Canada, 1888; asst.-under-sec. of state of Canada, 1909.

PEMBERTON, SHOLTO RAWLINS.—Ed. Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1882; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1883; admitted to the bar, Leeward Is., 1883; elected mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1885 to 1895; mem. educn. comsrr., 1886; of road bd., Dominica, 1889; nominated mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and mem. exec. coun., 1895; comsrr. of valuation, 1895; comsrr. of assessed taxes, 1896; J.P., 1896; chmn. bd. of guardians, 1897; vis. just. of gaol, 1897; mem. quarantine bd., 1898; nom. mem. legis. coun., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1898; ag. 1st puisne judge, Leewards, 1898; ag. asst. att.-gen., Dominica, 1899-1900; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1901 and 1902; asst. atty.-gen., 1903; ag. puisne judge, 1903-4-5-7-8-9 and 1910; ag. atty.-gen., 1905, 1908, 1909 and 1910; ag. chief just., 1911; ag. admstr. of Dominica, 1911; 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., Apr., 1911; 1st puisne judge, Mar. 1916; ag. chief just., May, 1916.

PENNEFATHER, CHARLES EDWARD DE FONBLANQUE.—B. 1885; clk., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Prot., 1908; clk. of peace, 1910; sub-inspr. of constab., Fiji, 1910; inspr., ditto, 1912; dist. comsrr., 1914; ag. dist. comsrr., Rewa, and gov. comsrr., Naitasiri, 1914-15; ag. dist. comsrr., Colo East, 1917-18; ag. ch. inspr., Fiji constab., ag. ch. pol. mag., regitr.-gen., regitr. of sup. ct., regitr. of titles, pub. trustee, regitr. of companies, cust. of enemy property, and cur. of intestate and vacant estates, 1918.
PENNEY, ALFRED.—M.H.A., Carbonear, Newfild., 1885-9; mem. exec. coun., and survr.-gen., 1885-9; judge dist. ct. Harbour Grace, 1898; stip. mag., Carbonear, 1900.

PEPPYS, WALTER EVELYN.—B. 1885; ed. at Malvern and Braemore Coll., Oxford, B.A., 1907; M.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1908; ag. assist. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1911; passed cadet, June 1911; cls. V., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Jelebu, Apr., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, June, 1913; ag. mag. cent. ct., Kelantan, and state auditor, Mar., 1914; on duty with Kelantan detachmt., Malay States Guides, 15th to 31st May, 1915; dist. offr., Pasir Putch, 1st June, 1915; ag. supt. of lands, Kelantan, and chmn., conservancy bd., Aug. to Dec., 1917; dist. offr., Pasir Putch, Jan., 1918; ag. supt. of lands, Kelantan and chmn., conservancy bd., in addition, May, 1918.

PERCEVAL, SIR WESTBY BROOK, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1854; was mem. for Christchurch City in legis. assem., of N. Zealnd; chmn. of comtees. in 1890; agt.-gen. for the col. in England, 1891-96; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1878; Roy. comsrr. for Chicago Exhibn., 1893; agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1896 to 1899.
PERCIVAL, ARTHUR BLAYNEY.—Asst. collr., R. Africa Prot., 14th June, 1900; ranger of game preserves, 10th May, 1901; senr. asst. game warden, 1910.

PERCY, FREDERICK WM.—B. 1857; ed. at private schl., Dublin; writer, Royal Navy, 1872; passed English civ. serv. exam., 2nd cls. clk., 1874; clk., wks. and rlyws. depts., W. Australia, 1892; minister's clk., wks. dept., 1896; ag. chf. clk., Jan., 1898; statistical clk., July, 1898; ag. chf. clk., Jan. to Dec., 1902; chf. correspondence clk., Mar., 1903; ag. chf. clk. for pub. wks., 14th Nov., 1908.
PERDUE, CECIL GRAHAM.—B. 1891; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; police probationer, Hong Kong, Nov., 1912; attached to Punjab police, Apr., 1915; temp. 2nd lieut., Indian army reserve, 27th Jan., 1916.

PERDUE, HON. WM. EGERTON, B.A.—B. 1850; ed. Brampton high schl. and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1879; called to the bar, Manitoba, 1882; judge, ct. of King's Bench, Manitoba, 1903; judge, ct. of appeal, Manitoba, 1904; chj. just. of Manitoba, 1918. Perez, Joseph Aldric, M.D., Edin.—B. 1860; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 6th Apr. 1887; dist. med. offr., 1st Mar., 1915.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—B. 1877; temporary clk. to auditor, B. Honduras, 6th Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treasy., customs and P.O. depts., 23rd June, 1893; 4th clk., 1st Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treasy. and cust. dept., 16th May, 1897; 2nd clk., 7th June, 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., 1st Sept., 1900; ag. chief clk., C.S.O., and clk. of councils, 12th Apr. to 30th Sept., 1901, 15th Mch., 1902, to 24th July, 1904, and from 14th Dec., 1904, to 28th June, 1905; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, 22nd July, 1905, to 14th Jan., 1906; ch. clk., C.S.O. and clk. of couns., 17th Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to gov. Sir E. Swayne, 16th Mch., 1909, to 31st July, 1910; ag. comsnr. of currency, 18th Feb., 1911; ag. treasur., collr. of cust., harb. mast., regiar. of shipping, chrmn. of bd. for examtn. of pilots, pres. of quarantine bd., chrmn. of poor relief committee, dir. of P.O.G., fund. mem. of bd. of educn.; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 9th Apr. to 13th Aug., 1911; J.P. for the Colony, 8th May, 1911, priv. sec. to ag. gov., 5th Aug., 1911.

PHILLIPS, HENRY DENBIGH.—B. 1872; super. num. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1887; ag. clk., col. engnr.'s office, 1888; ag. rev. offr., July, 1888, to Feb., 1889; clk. to att.-gen., May, 1889; ag. clk. to regiar. of sup. ct. and keeper of records, 1889, 1890, 1891, 1892, 1893 and 1894; clk. to regiar., sup. ct., 1894; comsnr. of sup. ct. and a comsnr. of deeds, Aug., 1894; ag. regiar. gen., Feb. to Sept., 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, Apr. to Oct., 1899; ditto, Toledo, Mar. to Dec., 1900; dist. comsnr., Toledo, 1st Jan., 1901; dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, 28th Aug., 1905; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize; offr. recvr. in bankruptcy and off. admstr., 11th Mar., 1907; ag. regiar. gen. in conjunction with duties as dist. comsnr., Apr. to Dec., 1907; and again from 5th Apr. to 22nd Oct., 1908; a comsnr. of currency, 24th Sep., 1908; ag. treas. and collr. of cust., harb.-master, etc., pres. quarantine bd., chrmn. of pauper relief comtee., 23rd Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize, etc., and regiar.-gen. 21st May to 12th Aug., 1909, and from 22nd Jan., 1910, to 3rd Dec., 1911; in charge of audit dept., 4th Dec., 1911.

PHILLIPS, THOMAS R.—Served in treasy., secretariat, customs, and post office, British Honduras, 1891 to 1900; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 22nd Aug., 1900; has acted on several occasions as chief asst. treas. and treas.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM LAMBERT COLLYER, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1858; B.A. (Dunelm); dep. clk., gen. assembly, Barbados, 1879; comsnr. of probate, 1882; clk. gen. assem., 1891; col. treas., 1892; ag. col. sec., July to Sept., 1911, May to Aug., 1912, and from May to Dec., 1913.

PHILP, HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1851; M.L.A. of Queensland, since 1886; sec. for pub. wks., 1893-6; sec. for pub. instr., 1894-5; for rly., 1895-7; for mines, 1893-9; treas., Mar., 1898, to Dec., 1899; premier, Dec., 1899, to Sept., 1903; and Nov., 1907, to Feb., 1908.
PICKERING, G. H.—Town mag., E.A.P., 1910; ag. 2nd puisne judge 1915; 2nd puisne judge, 1917.
PICKFORD, W. — Dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 21st Aug., 1909.

PICKWOAD, CECIL ARTHUR.—B. 1876; ed. Bedford Modern schl.; 3rd customsoffr., Br. Hond., June, 1895; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1895; several times acted as clk. in treasy., cust. and post off.; 3rd clk., treasy., and cust. and clk. to quarantine bd., May, 1897; 2nd clk., Sept., 1900; asst. auditor, N. and S. Nigeria, Dec., 1901; local auditor, G. Coast, Dec., 1902; local auditor, Lagos-S. Nigeria, May, 1905; ditto, new col. and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1906; ditto, Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914.

PICKWOAD, HOWELL.—Ed. at Bedford Mod. schl.; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Br. Hond., Nov., 1895; 3rd cust. offr., 12th Aug., 1898; 4th clk., treasy., and cust., and clk. to comsnrs. of currency, 27th Dec., 1901; 2nd grade cust. and excise offr., Aug., 1903; col. branch of exchequer and audit dept., 21st Oct., 1904; asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., 26th June, 1905.
PICKWOAD, HUGH FREDERICK.—B. 1878; comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1909; ditto (3rd div.), 1914.
PIDDINGTON, ALFRED BATHURST, K.C.—B. 1862; chief comsnr. of the interstate comsnr., Commonwealth of Australia, Aug., 1913.

PERIS, PAULUS EDWARD, Samarasinha Sriwardhana, M.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1895.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1896; asst. to govt. agt., Colombo, 1898; pol. mag., Panadura, 1899; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1899; asst. to govt. agt., Galle, 1901; ag. dist. judge, Matare, Mar., 1903; office asst. to govt. agt., Galle, June, 1903; asst. comsnr., St. Louis exhib., Feb., 1904; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1905; ditto, Kalutara, Mar., 1906; ag. regiar.-gen., Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1912; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Nov., 1913; dist. judge, Galle, June, 1914; ditto, Jaffna, Dec., 1915.

PIGGOTT, SIR FRANCIS TAYLOR, KT. BACH. (1905).—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc. gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; ch. just., Hong Kong, 27th Apr., 1905; ret., 1912; ree. thanks of coun. of govt. for ser. in connection with planters' loans, 1902; author of "Nationality and Naturalization," "Exterritoriality," "Extradition," "Foreign Judgments and Jurisdiction," "Law of Torts," and "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies."

PIGOTT, FRANCIS JOSEPH, M.I.C.E.—Dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1887; ag. mun. engrnr., Colombo, 1897; prov. engrnr., S. Prov., 1902; ditto, E. Prov., 1903; asst. D.P.W., 1904; dep. col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., Penang, S. Stittmns., 1905; ag. col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., S.S., Jan., 1907, to Apr., 1908; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Singapore, 1907; ag. col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., Sept., 1909; confirmed, Dec., 1909.
PIRIE, J. H. H., M.B., Bac. Surg., M.D., M.R.C.P.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

PITOT, LEON EMILE.—B. 1866; govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, 11th Apr., 1889; govt. engnr. and architect, Mauritius, Apr., 1891; gen. man. of rlyws. and engineer, Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896.

PITTMAN, EDWARD FISHER, A.R.S.M.—B. 1849; entd. mines dept., N.S. Wales, 1877; held positions of mining survr., geological survr., and chief mining survr.; lecr. in mining at Sydney Univ., 1893-1902; under-seo. of mines, N.S. Wales since Sept., 1902, and govt. geologist from 1901 to 1917; author of "The Mineral Resources of New South Wales," 1901, and many geological reports and papers.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC.—B.A., (Oxon); graduated, class. hona., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1901; off. astt., Kurunegala, 1902; off. astt., Galle, 1904; pol. mag., Avissawella, 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Apr., 1906; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1906; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1909; temporarily employed in C.O., London, May, 1915, to Dec., 1918; sec., oversea settlement comtee., Jan., 1919.
PLATTS, W.A.F.—Ast. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 15th Feb., 1907; dist. comanr., Apr., 1913.
PLOWMAN, CLIFFORD H. FITZHERBERT.—B. 1889; ed. at King's Schl., Ely, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1911 (class tripes); astt. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912.

PLOWMAN, Hon. GEORGE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1908).—Ck. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Natal, 1890; 1st ck., 1891; ch. ck., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. bd.; astt. under-seo. 1898; sec. to prime min., July, 1901; sec. lands and works (now rlyws. and harbrs.), Nov., 1901; mem., civ. serv. bd., 1901; sec. to prime min.; acctg. offr., rlyws. and harbrs., 1st July, 1905; prov. sec., Natal Prov., 1910; dep. adminstr., Natal Prov., Aug.-Nov., 1913, and from Aug., 1917; mem. prov. adminstr. comanr., 1915; adminstr., Natal, 1918.

PLUMER, GEN. SIR HERBERT CHARLES ONSLOW, G.C.B. (1818), G.C.M.G. (1916), G.C.V.O. (1917), A.D.C.—B. 1857; served in Soudan expdt., 1884, 4th cls., Medjidie, operations in S. Africa, 1896; S. African War, 1899-1902; A.D.C. to King Edward VII., 1901-2; Q.M.G. to the forces, 1904-5; comdg. 5th divn., Irish command, 1906-9; G.O.C.-in-C., Northern command, 1911-14; comdg. 2nd Army, British exped. force, France, 1915-17; G.O.C.-in-C., British forces in Italy, 1917; gov., Malta, 1919; gen., 1915; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, 1917; col., York and Lancs. Regt., 1917; Grand Officer Legion of Honour; Grand Officer, ss. Maur. and Laz. (Italy); Grand Officer, Order of Leopold, and Croix de Guerre (Belgium).
PLUMMER, E. A.—B. 1872; entered civ. serv., Trinidad, 12th Oct., 1892; 2nd ck. to regis. of sup. ct., 12th Feb., 1914.

PLUNKET, 5th BARON (created 1827), WM. LEE PLUNKET.—B. 1864; G.C.M.G. (1910); K.C.V.O., K.B.E., J.P.; Dublin and Wicklow; honorary attache to the embassy at Rome 1889-92; Constantinople, 1892-4; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland 1900-1904; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 1904-1910.
POLLONAIS, CHARLES ADRIEN.—B. 1862; entered civ. serv., Trinidad, 27th Mar., 1882; astt. rec-gen., 1st Apr., 1907.

POMARE, HON. MAUI, M.D., Chicago, U.S.A.—B. 1876; eduq. Boys' High Schl., Christchurch; govt. health offr. to maoris, 1901; entd. New Zealand parlmt., 1911; mem. of exec. coun., representing native race, 1912; also min. of Cook Islands.

POND, JOSEPH EDMUND.—Copyist, comanr.'s office, Montserrat, 1st June, 1907; 3rd ck. boarding offr., treasy. and customs dept., 16th July, 1910; ag. 2nd ck. and post office ck., Apl. to July, 1911; 4th ck. and ck. to valuation comanrs., Apl., 1912; ag. comanr.'s ck. and educational dist. offr., from 23rd June, 1912 to Jan., 1913; 3rd treasy. ck. and post office ck., Nov., 1912; ag. 1st ck., from 26th May, 1913.

PONTIFEX, SYDNEY CLAUDE OVERTON.—B. 1871; apptd. to cust., Br. Guiana, 1891; 5th class offr., 1892; 4th ditto, 1894; 3rd ditto, 1898; transf. to cust., So. Nig., 1899; ag. P.M.G., Apr., 1900; astt. collr. cust., Feb., 1901; ag. collr. and ag. P.M.G., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Nov., 1904; prov. collr. of cust., Oct., 1906.
POOLE, H.—B. 1887; apptd., after compet. exam., ck. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1907.

POPE, FREDERICK SIDNEY.—B. 1869; educ. Wellington Boys' Coll., New Zealand; served in govt. rly. dept., N. Zealand, 1896-1900; dept. of agric., 1900-1909; sec. for agriculture, industries and commerce, 1909-1918; astt. director-gen. of agric., 1918.

POPE, SIR JOSEPH, K.C.M.G. (1912), C.V.O. (1908), C.M.G. (1901), I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1854; entered civ. ser. of Canada in 1878 as priv. sec. to min. of marine and fisheries priv. sec. to the late Sir John A. Macdonald, prime min. from 1882 till his death in 1891; astt. ck. of the Queen's Privy Coun. for Canada, 1889; under-seo. of state and dep. registr.-gen. of Canada, 1896; author of the Memoirs of Sir John A. Macdonald; Royal Tour in Canada, 1901, and other works; attached to the staff of the Br. agt. on the Behring Sea arbitration at Paris, 1893; agt. of Canadian govt., at joint high coman., Quebec and Washington, 1898-99; deputed by govt. to arrange Royal tour in Canada, 1901; assoc. sec. to Alaska boundary tribunal, London, 1903; apptd., in association with Mr. Bryce, H.M.'s plenipotentiary at the International Conference on pelagic sealing, 1911; recd. C.V.O. for services on the occasion of the Quebec tercentenary celebrations, 1908; holds 2nd cls. of Japanese Order of the Sacred Treasure; under-seo. of state for external affairs, 1909.
POPE, PERCY W. T.—B. 1856; ed. at Prince of Wales Coll., Charlottetown, P.E. I.; apptd. Dominion auditor at Charlottetown, 1883; astt. rec-gen., 1st Jan., 1887.

POPE, TRAVELYAN ARNOLD.—B. 1860; B.A., Madras Univ., 1876; at Wren's Coll., London, 1877-1879; math. scholarship at Christ's Coll., Camb., 1879; senr. optime, 1882; mast. for two years at Oratory Schl., Birmingham; German and math. mast., Birkenhead Schl., 1884-1887; prof. of physics, Royal Coll., Mauritius, 1887-1904; principal, Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, since 1904; deleg. to agricultural and educational confce., at Barbados, 1908.

POPHAM, HENRY BRADSHAW, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; coman., 13th Light Infantry, 1900; served in S. African War (2 medals); seconded G. Coast Regt., 1906; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to ag. govr., G. Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1909; col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1910; priv. sec. to ag. govr., Feb. to June, 1911; ag. chief astt. col. sec. and ck. of councils, July-Aug., 1913; ag. senr. astt. col. sec., Oct., 1913; dist. comanr., Jan., 1914; seconded to Togoland political adminstr., Nov., 1914.

PORCH, MONTAGU PHIPPEN.—B. 1877; ed. at Bath Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; M.A., 1904; served with Middlesex Yeomanry in S. African war, 1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); Egyptian exploration fund with Prof. Flinders Petrie, 1904 and 1905; asst. res., Northern Nigeria, 14th July, 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Apr., 1912.

POORAL, JOHN.—Ck., port office, Gibraltar, Aug. to Sept., 1874, and July, 1877, to May, 1878; writer in H.M.'s dockyard, Sept., 1874, to July, 1877; 3rd elk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1878; 2nd elk., 1883; 1st class elk., Jan., 1890; ch. elk., Dec., 1891; asst. col. sec. and sec. to bd. of health, 1913.
PORTAL, E.—Asst. govt. med. officer, Seychelles, July, 1892; govt. med. officer, Mauritius (Rodrigues), 1901; asst. med. supt., Barkly, and lunatic asylums, 1st July, 1905.

PORTELLI-CARBONE, ALFONSO, M.D. (Malta Univ., 1883).—L. M. (Rot. Hosp., Dub.), 1890; police physician for Zurrico, Safi, Crendi, and assist. pol. phys. for Micabiba and Chiroo, and sanitary inspr. for the 6th country dist., Aug., 1884; dist. med. offr. for Valetta, Sept., 1885; is a mem. of the Br. Med. Assoc. (Mediterranean branch), 1888; fell. Br. Gynaeol. Soc., and mem. Intern. Med. Cong., Berlin, 1889, and Rome, 1894 (obst. sec.).

PORTER, W. HENRY, I.S.O. (1911).—Ck., pres.'s office, Dominica, Nov., 1874 treas. elk. and excise offr., Nevis, 1878; 1st landing waiter and quarantine offr., St. Kitts, May, 1881; ag. treas., Dominica, Feb., 1884, to Oct., 1887; ag. mag., dist. P., Nov., 1887, to Feb., 1888; treas., Dominica, July, 1890; adminstr. govt. of Dominica, Apr. to Sept., 1904, June, 1905, to Apr., 1906, Sept., 1910 to June, 1911; is a mem. of exec. and legis. couns. and chrmn. of quarantine board.

PORTER, WILLIAM THOMAS.—B. 1887; ed. at Univ. schl. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge B.A. (law), 1901; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1901; joined N. E. circuit, and pract. at York, Leeds, Bradford and West Riding of Yorks sessions; mag., E. Africa Prot., 10th May, 1907; ag. adminstr.-gen., July to Nov., 1907; town mag., Mombasa, Aug., 1907, to Sept., 1908; 1st cls. mag. for provinces of Kisumu and Naivasha, Sept., 1908, to Feb., 1909, town mag., Nairobi, 23rd Feb., 1909; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 26th Oct., 1911, to Mar., 1916; ag. pulmine judge, Cyprus, May, 1915, to Mar., 1916, and several previous occasions; pulmine judge, G. Coast, 14th Dec., 1915.

POTT, P. A. H.—B. 1877; asst. transport offr., Gold Coast, Sept., 1901; dist. comsrr., Ashanti, Jan., 1907; ag. cantonment mag., Coomassie, Oct. to Dec., 1907, and Apr.-May, 1909; ag. pol. mag., Coomassie, Nov. 1913 to July, 1914, and from Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comsrr., S. Prov., Ashanti, May and June, 1909, Sept. and Oct., 1913; ditto, W. Prov., Ashanti, Dec., 1910 to June, 1911, and from June to Dec., 1912.

POTTER, HENRY YARLE DEIGHTON.—B. 1875; ed. at Edinburgh Acad.; 2nd lieut., Edinburgh City Artil., July, 1894; lieut., Feb., 1896; passed in "Artillery," July, 1896; asst. engrnr., with Messrs. Blyth and Westland, consulting engrns. to North British Rly. Co., 1895; asst. engrnr. to Mr. John Strain, Glasgow, 1896; asst. engrnr., Caledonian Rly., 1897; draftsman, dir. of wks. dept., Admiralty, 1898; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 21st Nov., 1901; state engrnr.'s asst., Perak, 14th Feb., 1902; asst. engrnr., Larut and Matang, Perak, 1st Jan., 1905; exec. engrnr., 3rd grade, 1st Jan., 1906; ag. exec. engrnr., 2nd grade, Perak, June, 1906; ditto, Mar., 1909; ditto, Pahang, Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., grade II., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1913; seconded for mil. ser. by F.M.S. govt., 1914; lieut., R.G.A., 6th Jan. to 28th May, 1915; resumed duty in F.M.S., 11th Aug., 1915.

POTTER, THOMAS IRWIN.—Ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; ag. asst. elk., col. hosp., 18th Dec., 1882; clerical asst., Crown lands off., 10th Apr., 1883; elk., survey dept. (in addition to former post), 1st Dec., 1884; ch. elk., Crown lands off., 16th Aug., 1897; ch. elk. and asst. inspr. of schl., 1st Nov., 1898; warden, La Brea and Oropouche (owing to spec. knowledge of asphalt question); ch. offr. of customs, Brighton, La Brea, 1st Jan., 1899; harb.-master, Brighton harbour, La Brea, in addition to wardenship, 1st July, 1899; J.P. for county of St. Patrick; mem. of Royal society of arts, and dormant mem. Zoological society of Lond.; ag. sub. int., crown lands, 1st Sept. to 31st Oct., 1901, and from 10th Oct., 1905, to Jan., 1906; ag. harb.-mast., Colony, June to Dec., 1906; stip. justice, La Brea, and Oropouche in conjunction with wardenship, 5th June, 1907; ag. harb.-mast., Colony, May to Nov., 1908; ag. sub-intendant, Nov., 1908, to April, 1909; ag. administr.-gen., 28th Apr., 1909; ag. offr. recvr. and administr.-gen., 1st Feb. to 13th Dec., 1910; ag. regiar.-gen., 1st Apr. to 30th June, 1911; regiar.-gen. (provisionally), 1st July, 1911; confirmed, 16th Aug., 1912; mem. of Port-of-Spain town board, 10th June, 1913; nominated mem. of Port-of-Spain city council, 31st Oct., 1914.
POTTS, HENRY WILLIAM, F.L.S., F.C.S.—Prin. of Hawkesbury Agric. Coll., Richmond, N. S. Wales, since May, 1902.

POTTS, MOSES A.—Ed. S. Leone Gram. schl.; served in H.M.'s commissariat, S. Leone, June, 1853, till Jan., 1860; ag. 2nd elk., treas., July, 1865, to Jan., 1866; extra elk., Jan., 1866; house and land tax elk., Aug., 1869; 2nd elk., treas., after a compet. exam., Sept., 1870; census master, 1871; ch. elk. and cashier, 1877; ag. aud.-gen., Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1887; charity comsrr., 1890.

POUGNET, EDOUARD DESBARBIERS, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., Mauritius, Jan., 1888; asst. water authority, Moh., 1898; inspr. of water wks., July, 1903; govt. survr., Moh., 1911; supt. of pub. wks. and survr. of roads, St. Kitts, Apl., 1914; in service of French govt., in France during the war.

POUNTNEY, ARTHUR MECK.—B. 1873; ed. Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., 1896; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1903; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, 1904; ag. offr. assignee, 1906; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, 1906; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1909; supt. of census, F.M.S., 1910; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, 1912; treasr., S. Settmta., 1st Feb., 1914.

POWELL, HENRY.—Student, Royal Gardens, Kew, June, 1888, to Apr., 1890; curator, Botanic Gardens, St. Vincent, May, 1890, to Nov., 1903; asst. to dir. of agric., E. Africa prot., 7th Nov., 1903; ch. of economic plants div., Apr., 1907.

POWELL, HENRY TREVOR.—Ed. Monmouth schl.; apptd. 3rd asst. in treasr., Brit. E. Africa prot., 1st Dec., 1901; 2nd asst., 1st Apr., 1903; offr. mem., plague arbitn. bd., 1903; treasr., Somaliland prot., 28th Nov., 1903; supt. of post offices, Nov., 1906; G.A.S. medal and 2 clasps Somaliland, 1902-1904 and 1908-1910.

POWELL, ROBERT ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., assoc. Roy. Coll. of Science, Dub.—Dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Ceylon; ag. 2nd financial and office asst., Nov., 1892; dist. engnr., Oct., 1893; prov. engnr., Feb., 1914.

POWER, EDMUND LE POER.—B. 1872; asst. dist. comsnr., Somaliland Prot., 1901; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1903; ag. vice-consul, Zeyla, Oct., 1903; dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 1912; ag. prov. comsnr., E.A.P., 1915; Somaliland medals, 1902-1904, 1908-1910.
POWERS, HON. CHARLES—Justice of the high ct., of Australia, 1913.

POYNTON, HON. ALEXANDER.—Formerly M.H.A., S. Australia; mem. of house of representatives, Commonwealth of Australia; treas., C. of A., 14th Nov., 1916; ag. min. for the navy and min. in charge of shipping and shipbuilding Apl., 1918.

PRAIN, Lt.-COL. SIR DAVID, KT. BACH. (1912); C.M.G. (1912); C.I.E. (1906); M.A.; M.B., LL.D., F.R.S.E., F.L.S., F.R.S.—B. 1857; ed. Aberdeen gram. schl. and Univs. of Aberdeen and Edinburgh; demonstrator of anatomy, coll. of surgeons, Edin., 1882-3, and univ. of Aberdeen, 1883-1884; entd. Indian med. serv., 1884; curator, Calcutta herbarium, 1887-1898; prof. of botany, med. coll., Calcutta, 1895-1906; director, botanical survey of India, and supt. of royal botanic garden, Calcutta, 1898-1905; trustee of the Indian Museum, 1898-1904; sec. bd. of scientific advice for India, 1903-1904; mem. of coun. of various scientific societies; director of royal botanic gardens, Kew, since 1905.

PRANKERD, HORACE ARTHUR.—B. 1883; ed. at Ipswich and Queen's Coll., Cambridge (scholar, 2nd class Tripes), B.A., 1905; asst. master, Loretto, to July, 1907; then 2½ years at Lausanne and Freiburg-in-Baden Univs.; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; asst. res., 1911.

PRASAD, MUHARI.—B. 1888; Cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Point Pedro, Sep., 1913; addtl. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1914; addtl. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Dec., 1915; pol. mag., Chilaw, Apr., 1917.

PRATT, CHARLES E. J. BITH.—Ed. Trent Coll.; 2nd lieut., 4th batt. Sherwood Foresters Derbyshire regt., Jan., 1887; lieut., 4th batt. Prince of Wales North Stafford regt., May, 1888; instructor of musketry, Jan., 1890; lieut., Falmouth div. submarine miners, R.E., Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. Sierra Leone frontier pol., Aug., 1895; inspr., Apr., 1896; J.P. for S. Leone.

PRATT, EDWARD.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1899; ag. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, May, 1902; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1903; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Sept., 1903; ag. dist. treas., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1904; ditto, Tanjong Malim, Dec., 1904; ditto, Kuala Lipis, Aug., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1908; ag. mag., Taiping, Oct., 1910; asst. registr., supreme ct., Kinta, May, 1911; ag. revenue auditor, Perak, 23rd Oct., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 7th July, 1913; offr. of cls. III, S. Sttlmts., 21st Dec., 1914.

PRATT, F. G., A.M.I.O.E.; asst. engrn., Lagos govt. rly., Mar., 1907; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Uganda, Sept., 1908; ditto, P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Dec., 1911; represented N. Nigeria at 3rd International Road Congress held in London in June, 1913; dir. of pub. wks., Nyasaland, 1917.

PRENDERGAST, HON. SIR JAMES, KT. BACH. (1881).—B. 1828; is grad. of Univ. of Camb.; called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1857; admitted a barrister of the sup. ct. in N. Zealand, Dec., 1862; atty.-gen., 1866; ch. just. of N.Z., 1875-1899.

PRENDERGAST, HON. JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verandrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1902; ditto, Saskatchewan, 1907; puisne judge, court of King's Bench of Manitoba, 1910.
PRESCOTT, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Appd. after compet. exam., supplementary clk., supreme ct., Gibraltar, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1899; asst. registr. and deputy clk. of arraigns, 1911.

PREVOST, EDWARD WILLIAM DE TOERS.—B. 1882; served in S. Africa as 2nd lieut., 3rd Royal Lancaster regt., 1901-2 (Queen's medal with two clasps); wing offr. of constab., and supt. of civil pol., B. N. Borneo, 1904-1911; passed higher standard exam. in Malay language; passed course of instruction at R.I.C. depot, Dublin; asst. comsnr. of pol., Gold Coast, Apl., 1912; passed lower standard exam. in Fanti language; comsnr. of pol., Jan., 1915; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Apl., 1917.

PRICE, G. F.—Dist. acct., S. African govt. rly.s. (construction), Natal, 1904-1907; acctnt., Baro-Kano rly., N. Nigeria, Feb. to July, 1908; acctnt., P.W.D., 1st Aug., 1908; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, Oct., 1910; ag. chief assist. treas., Aug. to Nov., 1912.
PRICE, HERBERT HENRY.—2nd clk. to asst. comsnr., Northern Div., Bech. Prot., 1910; 2nd clk., res. comsnr., office, 1st May, 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1910.

PRICE, WALTER CROMWELL, B.A.—Ed. Rathmines Schl., Dublin, and at Dublin Univ. and Schl. of Engineering, Queen's Coll., Galway; asst. to James Price, A.M.I.C.E.; engaged on Galway harbour works, 1880; Lock Erne drainage, 1882; Irish light rly.s., 1883-1884; Bandon water wks., 1885-1886; dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1887; in charge of irrigtn. wks., 1888-1890; ag. prov. engrn., North-Central Prov., 1896; ditto, Uva Prov., 1902; ditto, Northern prov., 1902; prov. engrn., North-Western Prov., 1904; ditto, Central Prov., 1907; mem. of mun. coun., Kandy, and also of san. bd., Kandy, 1910; prov. engrn., W. Prov., Mar., 1914; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1915; ditto, N. Prov., Oct., 1916.

PRICHARD, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1873; ed. at Keble Coll., Oxford (open class scholar, 1892); called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1902; practised in London, 1902-1910; stip. mag., Trinidad, 19th Aug., 1910; puisne judge, Mauritius, 19th Feb., 1915; ag. procureur and advoc.-gen., 30th Oct., 1916, to 2nd July, 1917; chrmn. of coman. on Royal College, 1916-1917; seconded for service as chrmn., sugar sales comte., 1st Sept., 1918.
PRICHARD, WILLIAM OWEN.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 15th Feb., 1904; senr. med. offr., 1913.

PRINCE, EDWARD ERNEST, F.R.S.C., &c.—B. 1858; ed. Modern Schl., Leeds, and St. Andrews, Camb., and Edin. Univ.; apptd. demonstr., zool., Edin. Univ., 1885; naturalist, Scotch fishery bd. laboratory, St. Ands.; prof. of
PROBSTING, Hon. W. B., C.M.G. (1918).—M.L.C., Tasmania; atty.-gen. and min. for rly., Apr., 1916.

PROUT, W. T., C.M.G. (1905), M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—Asts. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1885; surg. supt., immigrant ship “Dundee,” 1887; asts. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Silla; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895; title altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and correspndg. mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect., schl. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. adviser to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and sanitary comttee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; author of article “Yaws” in “Diseases of Warm Climates” (Davidson); Fil. Voloulous (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); “Filariasis in S. Leone” (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.

PRYCE, HOWARD LLOYD, C.M.G. (1911).—Trav. comsnr., Gambia Prot., Oct., 1896; attached to Gambia field force, 1901 (medal and clasp); ag. ch. mag., Gambia, July to Oct., 1903; 1st cls. trav. comsnr., Jan., 1904.

PTOLEMY, Wm. JOHN, I.S.O., J.P.—B. 1850; ed. at Smithville grm. schl., Ontario; served with G.W. Rly. Co. of Canada, 1873-1875; C.P.R. Dominion govt. telegraph construction, 1875; mercantile life, 1876-1882; entd. govt. serv. as acctnt., treasry. dept., Manitoba, 1883 to 1885; municipal comsnr., 1886; dep. prov. treasr., since 1886.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hymer’s Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asts., dock engrn.’s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hessle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asts. dir. of pub. wks., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916.
PUGH, J.—Med. offr., E.A.F., Jan., 1910.

PUGHE, S. R.—B. 1877; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904; confi. clk., Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 1st Mar., 1916.

PUGSLEY, HON. WILLIAM, K.C., D.C.L.—B. 1850; educ. schls., Sussex, N.B., Univ. of New Brunswick, B.A. (1868), and Univ. of Lond., B.C.L., D.C.L.; called to the bar, N.B., 1872; reporter of the supreme ct., 1873-83; Q.C. by Dom. govt., 1891; elec. to legis., N.B., 1885; speaker, 1887; solr.-gen., 1890; resigned, 1892; re-entrd. legis., 1899; attorney.-gen., 1900; premier, 1907; sworn of the priv. council for Canada and min. of pub. wks., 1907; re-elec. to H. of C., Sept., 1911; resig. portfolio on defeat of Laurier govt., Oct., 1911; lieut.-gov. of New Brunswick, 1918.

PURCELL, SIR GILBERT KENHELM TREFFRY, Kt. Bach. (1916).—Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Line’s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; apptd. asts. to atty.-gen. Leeward Islds. (St. Kitts), Mar., 1898; official mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of the presidency of St. Christopher and Nevis; ag. atty.-gen. Leewards. Aug.-Oct., 1900; apptd. ag. Ist puisne judge, June, 1901; puisne judge, G. Coast, 11th June, 1901; chief justice, Sierra Leone, 1911.

PURDON, ANDREW.—B. 1875; ed. at Ebury and Conway colls., N. Wales; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway"; sub-lieut., R.N.R., Apl., 1905; lieut., 1908; qualified at Whale Island and H.M.S. "Vernor"; asst. marine supt., N. Nigeria Marine, 11th Sept., 1909; whilst on leave took special course of instruction in hydrographical survey at R.N. coll., Greenwich, 1911.

PURVES, JAMES McLENNAN.—Govt. forester, Nyasaland, May, 1900; ag. head, forestry and botanic dept., 1906; ch. forest offr., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., Aug., 1911, May, 1914, and Sept., 1918; chmn., food production and collection comttee., 1918.

QUARTEY, ROBERT EMMANUEL.—B. 1863; outdoor offr., customs, Accra, 1881; 4th clk., customs and treasy., 1883; 2nd clk., audit office, 1885; 1st clk., 1887; audit clk. to local auditor, Jan., 1889; has several times acted as local auditor; transf'd. to post and tel. dept., 1902; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., 1903; in charge of post and tel. dept., 1903; sent to Axim on spec. P.O. duty, 1903; on spec. duty at Pram Pram, 1904; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., 1905 and 1906; dist. postmtr., Jan., 1907; taking spec. course of instr. at G.P.O., London, 1907.

QUICK, HON. SIR JOHN, KT. BACH. (1901); LL.D.—B. 1852; elec. to first H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia, 1901; re-elec. in 1903 and 1906; postmtr.-gen., C. of A., June, 1908; retired from politics, 1913.

RADCLIFF, JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. Denstone and Royal Schl., Armagh; lieut., I.Y., S. African war, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); lieut. 5th Batt. Leinster Regt., 1903; D.S.C., N. Nigeria, 1906; senr. transport offr., 1908; ag. chief transport offr., Aug., 1909, to Feb., 1910; ag. cantonment mag., Zungeru, Jan., 1911 to July, 1912.

RADFORD, WILLIAM JOHN.—Charing Cross hosp.; M.R.C.S. (England), 1887; L.R.C.P. (London), 1890; F.R.G.S., 1900; hon. mem., St. John's ambulance soc., 1896; med. offr., K. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1898; sen. med. offr., 1910; chief sanitary offr., Apl., 1913.

RAE, JAMES STANLEY.—B. 1881; articled and conf. clk. to Sir W. R. Davies, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; ditto to Sir Anton Bertram, Kt., when atty.-gen., Bahamas; called to the bar, Bahamas, Apr., 1904; admitted as a student, Middle Temple, 1910 (attending terms on vacations); clk. to legis. coun., Bahamas, 1901-13; ag. coroner for the Is. of New Providence from 1904, confirmed in 1911; J.P., 1910; ag. atty.-gen. and advoc.-gen. admiralty advoc. and temp. mem. of the exec. coun., Jan., 1910; ag. stip. and circ. mag., 1910-11, and on other occasions; ag. pros. couns. for crown on several occasions; dist. comntr., Belize, B. Honduras, from 1914; offl. rec. in bankruptcy, and offl. admstr. and J.P. for the col., 1914; regis.-gen., Nov., 1915, in conjunction with duties as dist. comntr.; dir. of P.O.G. fund, 1916; custodian of enemy property, Sept., 1916.

RAINIER, GEORGE DIRK.—Temp. clk., mag's. off., Prince Albert, Cape Colony, July, 1881; clk., Oudtshorn, Apr., 1882; auditor of expenditure accts., C.S.O., Feb., 1886; ditto, atty.-gen's. off., 1889; auditor and acctnt. of rev., June, 1891; asst. mag., Malmesbury, Feb., 1892; asst. mag., Somerset West, 1896; ag. civ. comntr. and res. mag., for numerous districts; civ. comntr. and res. mag., Barkly West, Aug., 1900; and Middelburg, 1904; taxing master and asst. regist., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1906; also high sheriff for Cape Colony, 1908; insp'tg. mag. and insp'r. of prisons, Apr., 1912; under sec. for justice, Union of S. Africa, July, 1912; ag. mem., public service comn., Sept., 1917.
RAINSFORD, R. F.—Asst. supt. of police, E.A.P., Apr., 1914.

RALPHS, EDWIN, F.R.G.S., F.C.S., F.E.L.S., M.R. San. Inst.—B. 1872; ed. St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. in Queen's scholarship, in final teachers' exams. (education dept.), and in archbishops' divinity exam.; honours in science, South Kens.; asst. master (junior) Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1898; senior grade, Normal mast., 1906; ag. second master, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, and Apr., 1908 to Apl., 1909; 2nd mast., 8th Apr., 1909; ag. head mast. and mem. of advisory comttee., Tech. Inst., 5th July to 5th Nov., 1909, and from 11th May, 1910, to 25th Oct., 1911; ag. mast., pol. schl., Hong Kong, May, 1902, to Mar., 1904; hon. examnrr. in chemistry to Hong Kong Coll. of Med., 1901, 1902 and 1903; dir., Hong Kong Technical Inst., from 1907; also mem. of exam. comttee. for "Teachers' Classes," Tech. Inst., 1909-1911; J.P., 1910; insp'r. of English schla., 1913; offr. in charge, St. Johns Ambulance Brigade, Hong Kong and China dist., 1915.
RALSTON, JAMES, M.A.—B. 1890; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1914; lecturer, technical inst.

RAM, GEORGE EDWARD—Lieut., 4th batt., Prince of Wales's, North Staffordshire Regt.; served in S. Africa, Oct., 1902, to July, 1903 (medal, with 3 clasps); ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Newfoundland, 9th Sept., 1903.

RAMANATHAN, PONNAMBALAM, C.M.G. (1889), K.C.—B. 1851; ed. at the Acad., Colombo, and Pres. Coll., Madras; barr.-at-law, Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. ct., Ceylon, 1875; mem. of the legis. coun., 1879; mem. of the lawcom. apptd. to report upon certain codes dealing with the laws of Ceylon, 1879; served also on the comn. apptd. at his instance to report upon the Thoroughfares Ordinance, 1881; obtained the introduction of P.O. savings bank in Ceylon, 1881-83; was one of the select comttee. of the legis. coun. apptd. to report upon retrenching the public expenditure of the island, 1882-83; a mem. of the coun. of legal educn., 1884; mem. of the Ceylon comn. of the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. of the Cent. Irrig'n. Bd., 1888; and of the select comttee. to report upon the incidence of the grain taxes, 1889; mem. of the comn. to inquire into the extension of the rly. to the N. parts of the island, 1890; solr.-gen. of Ceylon, 1892; ag. atty.-gen., 1894; ret., 1906; elected M.L.C., Ceylon, 1911; re-elected, 1917.
RAMSBOTTOM, HON. A. E. W.—B. 1860; formerly col. treas., O.R.C.; prov. admstr., O.F.S., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

RANFURLY, 5TH EARL OF (SIR UCHTER JOHN MARK KNOX, G.C.M.G.), Viscount Northland and Baron Welles of Dungannon, co. Tyrone, Ireland, Baron Ranfurly of Rampolhrie, co. Renfrew, United Kingdom.—Born 1856; succeeded his brother 1875; ed. Trinity Coll., Cambridge; J.P., D.L. for co. Tyrone; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; lord-in-waiting to the Queen, 1896-7; governor of New Zealand, 1897-1904.

RANKINE, RICHARD SIMS DONKIN.—B. 1875; clk. treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir George O'Brien, Sept., 1897, to 1901; acted clk. to ex. coun. Sept., 1899, to Mar., 1900: apptd. acot. native taxes, treasury, 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902; priv. sec. to Sir E. im Thurn, 10th Oct., 1904; conf. sec., 1st Jan., 1905; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific; clk. of exec. coun.; ag. sec., W. Pacific high commn., June, 1905 to June, 1906, and from Nov., 1907, to 31st Dec., 1908; asst. col. sec. and asst. recvr.-gen., 1st Jan., 1909; recvr.-gen. and commr. of stamps, pres. of marine bd., mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1910; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1913 to Nov., 1914; gov't's dep., 5th to 14th June, 1914; commr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. agt.-gen. of immigrn. (conjunct), Mar., 1915; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1915 to May, 1916; gov't's deputy, Aug., Oct., Dec., 1915; ag. col. sec., 18th Sept., 1917, to 6th Jan., 1918, and from 16th June to 9th Oct., 1918.

RANNIE, CLARENCE RUPERT.—B. 1872; clerical assist., audit dept., Antigua, May, 1889; clk. to col. engr., Antigua, Mar., 1891; ag. clk. to audr.-gen., Sept., 1894; 2nd clk. to audr.-gen., Leeward Is., Jan., 1895; audit clk., Dominica, Mar., 1897, to Aug., 1900; cashier, treas., Antigua, Sept., 1900; ag. 2nd outdoor offr., treas., 1st Aug. to 31st Oct., 1902; 2nd indoor offr., treas., 14th Nov., 1904; ag. 1st indoor offr., 23rd Nov., 1904, to 20th July, 1905, 16th May to 15th Aug., 1908, 1st May to 31st Oct., 1909, and from 1st to 31st Aug., 1910.

RASON, HON. SIR CORNTHWAITE HECTOR, Kt., Bach. (1909).—B. 1859; ed. at Brighton, Eastbourne and Reading; M.L.C. for Swan, W. Aust., 1889; M.L.A. for S. Murchison, 1897-1901; ditto for Guildford since 1901; min. of works, 1901; min. of works and rly., 1902-4; prem. of W. Aust., 1905; pres. of Royal commn. on mining, 1898, and of Royal commn. on immigrn., 1906; agt.-gen. in London for W. Aust., 1906-1909.
RAYNE, H. A.—Aast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1914.

READ, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B. (1914), C.M.G. (1907).—B. 1863; M.A., Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (afterwards Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; Ist clk., Oct., 1898; principal clk., 1st Apr., 1905; British delegate at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1899; one of the Brit. delegates on boundy. comm. at Berlin for the settm. of Anglo-German frontier in E. Africa, 1906; one of the Brit. plenipotentiaries at African liquor confce. at Brussels, 1906; mem. of hon. man. comm. of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908; chmn. of deptmtl. comm. to enquire into W. African med. staff, 1909; visited E. Africa, 1911-12; asst. under-sec. of state, 10th March, 1916; registr. of the Order of Saint Michael and Saint George.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne.—B. 1865; apptd. vet. to New Zealand govt., 1897; ch. vet., 1908; lisut.-col., N.Z. vet. corps, 1908; colonel, 1915; dir. of vet. services and remounts, N.Z. defence dept., 1908; dir., live stock div., agric. dept., 1909; dir.-gen. of agric., 1918.

REAL, HON. PATRICK.—B. 1847; called to the bar, Queensland, 1874; frequently acted as dist. ct. judge, and for some years was Crown proser. in cent. dist.; mem.of Royal commn. on establishment of a Queensland Univ., 1891; judge of sup. ct., Queensland, 1890.

REDINGTON, DUDLEY CECIL.—B. 1883; ed. Clongowes Coll., Ireland; Natal police, 1903-11; Zanzibar police, 25th March, 1911; supt., 15th Mar., 1912; asst. comdt., 1st Feb., 1914; ag. comdt. of police and ag. gov. of prisons, 7th Oct., 1915 to 31st Nov., 1916.

REED, HATHORNE.—B. 1873; M.A., Cantab.; ed. Bath Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; Western Circuit; lieut., S. African Constabulary, 1902-1908; 2nd mag., Zanzibar, Dec., 1908; ag. asst. judge, June-Dec., 1910; 1st mag., 8th Dec., 1910; ag. asst. judge, 9th Mar. to 28th Aug., 1915; 21st Apr. to 4th Nov., 1916; and from 25th June, 1917; author of "A Commentary on the Moneylenders' Act, 1909," and "A Commentary on the Police Offences Ordinance, 1902, of the Orange River Colony."
REED, JOSEPH MARTIN.—I.S.O. (1903); B. 1857; entd. public service of Victoria, 1875; survr.-gen., 1899-1914; sec. to dept. of lands.
REEP, ALFRED MILLS.—B. 1896; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1914.

REES, LEWIS CHARLES MORRIS PARKER.—B. 1876; ed. Kingston Coll., Yeovil and Merchant Venturers Tech. Coll., Bristol; art. pupil to Messrs. Foster and La Trobe, architects and survrs., Bristol, 1892; asst. to borough engrnr., Islington, 1897; ch. asst. borough engrnr., Margate, 1900; prin. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1902; also sec., squatters bd., 1905; lieut., Hong Kong vol. corps., 1909.

REEVE, HENRY, C.M.G. (1900). A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1854; passed C.S. exam., Victoria, 1868; instdl., Melbourne Univ., 1870; cadet, Victorian survey dept., 1873; cadet, N.S.W. survey dept., 1874; survr., 1878; survr., Fiji survey dept., May, 1880; staff survr., Jan., 1881; ag. commr. for wks., July, 1884 to Feb., 1885; col. engrnr., St. Lucia, 1887; dir. of survey, Windward Is., 1891; mem. legis. coun., St. Lucia, 1891; special mem. exec. coun., St Lucia and St. Vincent, 1891; col. engrnr., Gambia, 1894; mem. exec. coun., 1895; ch. English commr., Anglo-French bndry. comm., 1895-6, 1898-9; director of pub. wks., Lagos, Aug., 1901; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May; ag. gov't., May to July, 1902; ag. col. sec., 1902 and 1903; ag. gov't., 1902; deputy gov't., 1903; ret., 1904.

REEVES, FREDERIC WILLIAM.—B. 1876; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, Christ's Coll., Camb., and Wren's Inst.; Barbados Schl., 1895; math. scholar, Christ's Coll., 1896; B.A. (senr. time), 1898; M.A., 1906; asst.-inspr. of schools, Grenada, 1903; unofficial mem. of advisory commttee of bd. of dir. of secondary educn., July, 1906; ag. inspr. of schls., Grenada, Dec., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ditto, St. Vincent, May to July, 1907; ag. ch. clk., audit office, Grenada, Dec., 1907, to Jan., 1908; ag. inspr. of schls., Grenada, Jan.-Feb., 1908; headmaster, gram. schl. and inspr. of schls., St. Vincent, Aug., 1908; mem., bd. of educn., Sept., 1908.

REEVES, HON. WILLIAM PEMBER.—Born Canterbury, N.Z., 1857; ed. in Colony; prov. scholarship holder, 1868 and 1872; N.Z. scholarships in classics and English, 1874; Somes scholar, Christ's Coll., Canterbury; first elected mem. of H. of R., 1887; joined Ballance Min., Jan., 1891, holding the portfolios of educn., just. and labour; contd. in off. in Seddon Min. to Jan., 1896; agt.-gen. for N.Z. in London, 1896; represented N.Z. at Internat. Commer. Congress at Philadelphia, 1899; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtee., 1899; high commr. for N.Z. in London, June, 1905; resigned, 1908, on apptnt. as director of London schl. of economics; mem. of senate, London Univ.; author of the "Long Wh... Cloud," "State Experiments in Australia and New Zealand," and other publications relating to N.Z.
REFORD, JOHN HOPE, B.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O. (R.U.I.), L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liverpool).—Civ. surg., South African Field Force, 1901-2; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Aug., 1907.
REID, ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1904).—Ent. the govt. ser., Tasmania, 1858; under treasurer, 1895.

REID, ARTHUR WILLIAM.—Ed. at Leicester and Stafford; asst. store dept., Mersey rly., Liverpool, Jan., 1886; chief in 1889; asst. loco. supt. (stores), Uganda rly., 1897; sen. asst., 1899; chief storekeeper, 1903.
